programme.title: Where the Heart Is

programme.desc: Left to look after Felsham Hall until it has been sold, Lovejoy uses the house as a furniture showroom. It seems unlikely anyone could replace Lady Jane in the dodgy dealer's affections - but that doesn't prevent his eye being caught by glamorous auctioneer Charlotte Cavendish. Charlie Gimbert eventually buys the property, and agrees to rent the stables for Lovejoy Antiques. Patricia Hayes guest stars in an episode directed by Ian McShane.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dodgy dealer is threatened by a debt collector over a long outstanding sum and his hopes of meeting the repayment rest on a silver tankard, and hijacking a local auction administered by Charlotte Cavendish to obtain an antique piece of Japanese pottery. Meanwhile, newcomer Beth Taylor gives Eric and Tinker a helping hand. Ian McShane stars.(n)

programme.title: Sharpe
programme.desc: Having finally recovered from the death of his beloved Teresa, Sharpe marries Jane Gibbons. Before the honeymoon is over, he is forced to rush off and capture a fort behind French lines - and while he is away a fever outbreak threatens to cut short his new marriage. Sean Bean stars.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: Pru from the Embassy visits Barry in jail and reassures the gang they are doing everything they can to free him - but the Cubans have discovered his Russian connections and convinced themselves he is a spy. Neville feels the pressure as he is forced to home in on his target, and Oz faces a major setback in his relationship with Ofelia. Timothy Spall, Jimmy Nail, Tim Healy and Kevin Whately star.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: A terrified Neville is kidnapped, driven through the back streets of Havana and deposited at an empty house - where he is greeted by Pru from the Embassy, who asks him to trap rogue operative Tarquin. Meanwhile, there is still no sign of Barry being released from jail, and his deteriorating condition prompts Oz to make a heartbreaking decision. Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail star.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe

Clues point to the husband - but the duo suspect it could be part of a conspiracy. Adapted by Alan Plater from Reginald Hill's award-winning crime novels, with Ralph Brown and Susannah Corbett.(n)

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: A couple stumble upon the body of an elderly man in the old hospital wing of an abandoned mental health facility, strapped to a gurney with electrodes connected to his temples.(n)

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: A serial arsonist targets sites related to the 1983 general election. After risking his life in a burning building following a quiet word from Keats, Ray gets personally involved in the high-profile investigation - which forces him to face his traumatic past as it becomes clear the tragic events of the Falklands War are driving the firestarter to murder. Fantasy drama, with Dean Andrews and Joe Absolom (Doc Martin).(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene jeopardises a covert operation into a drug cartel when he learns several dealers have been murdered recently and the crimes are linked to an old adversary. Chris falls for the attractive undercover officer, but his feelings prompt a violent outburst that threatens his future in the police, and Alex gathers more clues about why she remains in the 1980s. With guest star Zoe Telford (Criminal Justice).(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry and the team agree to help a man who believes his wife is still alive, even though she apparently died 18 months earlier in a car accident. They decide the best way to convince him is to have her exhumed - only to be faced with more questions than answers when it turns out the body is not hers after all. Meanwhile, Brian needs to find a handyman when Esther bans him from his latest DIY project. Hugh Dennis and Samuel West guest star, with Amanda Redman.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reopens the investigation into an actress's disappearance when clips of her being assaulted in a 1990 movie are posted on the internet. Director Don Maddox features in the footage, but when questioned, he appears to have problems distinguishing fact from fiction. A potential witness is then found dead, and Brian and Jack are seconded to the unit handling the case, only to find Emily Driscoll (Dennis Waterman's daughter Hannah) is heading the operation. Imogen Stubbs and Sandra Dickinson also guest star.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad reinvestigates the death of a timeshare magnate, previously thought to have committed suicide, when a reformed call girl claims she was with him the night he died and saw someone force their way into his house as she left. It then emerges the deceased's widow has since fallen on hard times after being swindled by her late husband's business partner - who is connected to a notorious criminal. Guest starring Shane Richie, Naomi Bentley and Claire Rushbrook.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Amber continues to tease Dan about his love life. Goaded by her constant jibes, he reluctantly admits to dating a married woman - much to the anger of Rochelle. Zain looks into the case of an Egyptian girl who has been reported missing twice in one day. Leela makes a grisly discovery, and Jo Masters goes undercover to investigate a series of drug overdoses at a private prison - only to encounter a figure from her past.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Jo Masters goes undercover as a warder at Stonewall prison to investigate the source of drugs which have led to a spate of overdoses. Yvonne Hemmingway comes across an old flame involved in a shooting case and is forced to arrest him for concealing vital evidence, while Dan Casper's affair with commander's wife Rochelle Barratt is strained when they clash over a professional matter. Pauline Quirke guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Reg Hollis and Tony Stamp observe drugs being brought into Stonewall women's prison through the visitors' hall as corrupt officers turn a blind eye. Jo Masters realises Cath Wilson has used her to establish herself as top dog on the wing and sets out to get revenge. Yvonne Hemmingway is sorely tempted when her ex Nick pleads for a second chance. Pauline Quirke guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Zain Nadir finds a witness to the Garrison shooting, but inept interference from DI Markham scuppers the case and puts Dale Smith in grave danger as he searches for the gun at Larson's home. Amber Johannsen rumbles Dan's affair with drug referral officer Rochelle Barratt and is warned in no uncertain terms to keep her mouth shut.(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Robert tries to arrange a love-match between Eleanor and one of his old comrades-in-arms, but meets with a conspicuous lack of success. Then a belligerent boxer with head injuries gives him an unexpected helping hand, and medicine starts taking a back seat to romance at the Thrift. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave, David Calder and Nicholas Farrell.(n)

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Eleanor unwittingly saves the life of a brutal East End villain, who begins terrorising patients and staff alike. Period drama, starring Jemma Redgrave.(n)

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Girl
programme.desc: Feature-length period drama, starring Siobhan Flynn. A dying woman takes her daughter to the home of her former lover, a wealthy mine agent, who takes the child in. But the man's wife is less than happy about the arrangement and determines to keep the girl apart from her own offspring. With Jonathan Cake, Mark Benton and Susan Jameson.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Regina and George return to Ash Park from their wedding, and are warned about their marriage of convenience by Elizabeth and Carolyn. Meanwhile, Sarah attempts to arrange Rene's funeral and is offered emotional and practical support by Roy and Doris Collins. Elsewhere, Gino accidentally finds and reads Ann's novel, and Elizabeth is invited to a function in Sydney by Douglas Goddard.(n)

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George consummates his marriage to Regina after she spikes his drink with morphine, and warns her it will never happen again. Sarah returns to work at the hospital, although Jack worries it is too soon after Rene's death, and Douglas invites Elizabeth to accompany him on holiday.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Relishing the social prestige it confers, Hyacinth decides that she and husband Richard should have a weekend at a golfing hotel so that she can keep up with the Major and his wife. Roy Clarke's suburban sitcom, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth is thrown into dismay when her beloved father goes to the register office to be married, and things go from bad to worse when her neighbour Emmett comes face to face with her uncouth relatives, Daisy, Onslow and Rose.(n)

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth dreads the idea of attending Onslow's birthday celebrations. However, she changes her mind when she learns a limousine will be arriving to take her to the party. Starring Patricia Routledge, Clive Swift and Geoffrey Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne oversees the opening of a family planning clinic at the local community centre, leading mother-of-three Wilma Goddens to start taking the contraceptive pill behind her husband's back. Barbara is distraught when she discovers that her father has accepted a missionary posting in New Guinea for the next three years so will not be able to officiate at her wedding, while Violet suffers terribly with hot flushes, Christopher asks Trixie to meet his daughter, and Delia's heartache may finally be over.(n)

programme.title: Harrow
programme.desc: Fairley and Harrow are sent to the Outback to investigate a multiple-death car collision in Fairley's home town of Eurella Creek. When Fairley finds out that Harrow slept with Grace, he bluntly tells him he has a tendency to ruin people's lives. Harrow also learns that Fairley is a local hero for rescuing three children from a house fire in 1991, but still feels guilty about failing to rescue the fourth one. Maxine confronts Grace about not facing the demons of her past. Australian drama, starring Ioan Gruffudd.(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: The team is drawn into the world of a strict religious sect when a father and son are murdered with copies of the Bible at their sides. Burke grows increasingly irate as the secretive community closes ranks, refusing to speak to non-members, and he suspects the leader James Hardie knows more about the killings than he is letting on. Meanwhile, Reid investigates the disappearance of an old friend's daughter and uncovers a link to Burke's case. Detective drama, guest starring Sandy Morton, with Alex Norton and Blythe Duff.(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Pathologist Duncan Clark (Davood Ghadami) begins work with Burke and Reid on the case of a tortured and mutilated body found in a vacant warehouse. After identifying the victim as newly qualified doctor Scott Clarkson, they discover he may been involved in a drugs scam, but the investigation is complicated by the unexpected arrival of London cops DI Casey and DS Moretti. Glasgow-set detective drama, guest starring Reece Dinsdale (Coronation Street) and Steve John Shepherd (Plus One), with Blythe Duff, John Michie and Alex Norton.(n)

programme.title: Dalziel & Pascoe
programme.desc: Dalziel witnesses a shooting through a neighbour's window and rushes to the scene to find a dead woman, whose husband is suspected of being responsible for the incident. Feature-length drama, starring Warren Clarke and Colin Buchanan, with Michael Kitchen and Josette Simon.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Gerry celebrates his birthday at a comedy club, where a controversial act comes under fire from a group of hecklers. Sandra discovers the most vocal member of the gang is the niece of a political activist who went missing 25 years previously after waging a campaign against the comedian. Agreeing to reinvestigate the disappearance, the squad discovers that the activist and her boyfriend had infiltrated an extreme right-wing group with links to the dangerous criminal who murdered Jack's wife. Guest starring Phil Daniels, Joe Absolom and John Henshaw.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The arrest of a Turkish girl who has been working illegally leads Ucos to reopen the case of Richard Gibson, a pub landlord who died in a fire in 2009. The original investigation concluded Gibson's death was probably suicide due to his dire financial situation, but the detectives have some questions about the pub's live-in barmaid, who disappeared on the night of the blaze. Could widowed landlady Joanne, pub regular Jason, or the dead man's best friend David, who happens to have a sideline in passing off his British-produced sparkling wine as French champagne, supply them with any answers? While the case tests the team's brain power, Sasha is also keen to raise their fitness levels as she signs them up for a five-a-side football competition. programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Pembrokeshire with a couple who want not only to buy their first house together, but also need accommodation for holidaymakers and a horse.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various animals go undercover in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. Spy Pup is immersed in wild dog life to explore their maternal love, and Spy Penguin records the turbulent action of the mating season, as a male Adelie penguin falls victim to a pebble thief that ruins his chances of love for the season. Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Scandal at the Post Office - Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian arranges a memorial for Dennis and Sharon is touched by the effort he has put in. Gray finds Whitney disorientated on the street and takes her back to his place, where they go over the night Leo was killed. Mick and Linda have their eyes on a house to rent, but they are worried about Phil paying them with dodgy money for The Vic. Peter asks Dotty to fix his car but she refuses.(n)

programme.title: Sitting In Limbo
programme.desc: After living in the UK for 50 years, Anthony Bryan is called in by his manager at the care home where he works and told she has received a letter from the Home Office claiming that he has no right to work in the UK and is suspending him. A few months later, immigration officers arrive at Anthony's front door and he is put into a van and taken to a detention centre in Dorset. Drama inspired by a story from the Windrush scandal, starring Patrick Robinson, Nadine Marshall and Pippa Bennett-Warner.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Up-and-coming writer Arabella returns from visiting her boyfriend in Italy and is greeted by a deluge of frantic messages from her literary agents. With a matter of hours left to finish a draft of her second book, she plans to pull an all-nighter - but is soon tempted to join a friend on a night out. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, created by and starring Michaela Coel, with Weruche Opia and Paapa Essiedu.(n)

programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and guest presenter Ben Shires help three children to plan a game show-themed wedding, featuring embarrassing questions for their parents.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson plays clips featuring an angry, dinosaur-like creature on the warpath and a lemming terrorising a cat.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The most unusual places in the UK.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.sub-title: Blue Peter: Disney Superstar and David Walliams
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores habitats shared by humans and dangerous creatures, and helps remove a venomous snake from the garden of a family in South America.(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist goes on a grand tour of the planet to explain how the Earth's beauty is created by just a handful of forces, beginning by examining the diversity of shapes in the natural world. Off the coast of Canada, Brian explains how massive icebergs that surge down from Greenland and into shipping lanes of the Atlantic emerge due to a powerful yet infinitesimal force of nature, while revealing how honeycombs made by bees in Nepal to store their precious honey conceal a mathematical rule.(n)

programme.title: Alexander and the Terrible, Horrible, No Good, Very Bad Day (2014)
programme.desc: An 11-year-old boy has a day where everything seems to go wrong, but gets no sympathy from his family. He becomes convinced that bad things only ever happen to him, but then his parents, his brother and his sister all suffer terrible days of their own. Family comedy based on Judith Viorst's series of children's books, starring Ed Oxenbould, Steve Carell and Jennifer Garner.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for the Factory
programme.desc: Time moves on for the volunteers from 1973 to 1976, when disco was dominating the dance floors and maxi dresses were in vogue. It was also a time of great hope for women, with the Sex Discrimination Act having just been passed and the Equal Pay Act now in force. But will the changes in the law lead to equality on the factory floor or will the workers' hopes be dashed once again?(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain explores the culinary traditions of the Peak District and surrounding areas, beginning by putting her own twist on the classic Bakewell tart. She then joins Ian Barrett to learn how to make Derbyshire oatcakes, before using them as a casing for her potato and dill samosas.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the six remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room to revive the glamorous, and sometimes ridiculous style, of the 1980s. Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to get down to business with the trickiest pattern so far, a woman's power jacket, before they are tasked with turning high-vis workman clothes into a party outfit in the Transformation Challenge. Finally in the Made to Measure Challenge, the contestants create their own versions of a cocktail dress.(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: The Final Fix
programme.desc: Architect Piers Taylor revisits people he and Kieran Long followed as they built their homes from scratch for less than £100,000, and helps them complete their projects. Sue and Tim still need an entrance hall staircase as a centrepiece to transform their home, but they require a series of low-cost lessons to have a chance of achieving high-end results. In north-east Scotland, Marcus and Sholto want to create outbuildings to stand alongside their rural home, and Piers presents them with a design that takes inspiration from a building on the banks of Loch Doine.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of film and television appearances made by actor and dancer Gene Kelly over the years. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Carousel (1956)
programme.desc: A criminal struggling to support his pregnant wife dies during a botched armed robbery, but is given the chance to return to life 15 years later to help his family and teach his daughter a valuable lesson. Adaptation of Rodgers and Hammerstein's musical, starring Gordon MacRae, Shirley Jones, Cameron Mitchell, Barbara Ruick and Gene Lockhart.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: To get some tips for completing a complex fisherman's jumper, Ruth Goodman heads to pretty Polperro, and Tessa Dunlop takes the plunge into the glamorous history of British lidos.(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall discovers how the people and animals of Dartmoor live alongside each other, visiting an ancient hill farm housing dozens of house martins and swallows.(n)

programme.title: Cricket: England v West Indies Greatest Matches
programme.desc: A look back at the memorable 1984 Test from Lord's. An England side containing the likes of Ian Botham and Bob Willis took on a Windies team that cemented their reputation during the series as one of the greatest of all time.(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Private Pike receives his call-up papers and unexpectedly passes his medical with flying colours, much to the horror of his mollycoddling mother. Classic comedy first aired in 1975, starring Ian Lavender, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's tour reaches its final stop, Singapore, where he learns about the island city's maritime origins and the vision of the 19th-century Englishman who put it on the map. At the bar of the Raffles Hotel, he treats himself to a Singapore Sling, now a world-famous cocktail, takes a boat down the river and dines with an artist and his family in Chinatown.(n)

programme.title: Eric Clapton: Life in 12 Bars
programme.desc: Documentary profiling the blues guitarist, narrated by the man himself, as he charts his six decades in the music business, and why his insatiable desire to grow his artistic voice led him to quit a stream of successful bands, from the Yardbirds and John Mayall and the Bluesbreakers to short-lived supergroups Cream and Blind Faith. Clapton also talks about his battles with drugs and alcohol, his love for George Harrison's wife Pattie Boyd and the tragic loss of his son in an accident. Including archive interviews with BB King, George Harrison and Jimi Hendrix.(n)

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Including electric performances by the Rolling Stones, Eric Clapton, David Bowie, Motorhead, Nazareth, AC/DC and George Thorogood, as well as acoustic material by Michael Chapman, Horslips and Paco de Lucia.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows (2014)
programme.desc: A documentary crew follows the daily lives of four vampires sharing a house in New Zealand as they prepare for an annual gathering of the undead. A new member of the household introduces the bloodsuckers to modern life, but his failure to follow the laws of their kind brings his flatmates nothing but trouble. Horror comedy, directed by and starring Jemaine Clement and Taika Waititi.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's culinary archive continues as he reaches the letter M. Jamie Oliver prepares a mozzarella and chilli salad and the Hairy Bikers cook moussaka.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: John Craven is at home in his garden, where he gives tips on what butterflies to look out for and how to get involved in a nationwide survey. He also catches up with NHS fundraiser Tom Moore to discover what role the countryside has played in his life. Shepherdess Hannah Jackson puts her two new collie pups through their first paces as working dogs, and Adam Henson turns teacher as he takes his piglets to the country's home-schooled children.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don adds a variety of herbs that thrive in shade to the herb garden and adds summer annuals to the cottage garden. Nick Bailey has taken a rare opportunity to design his town garden from scratch and gives tips on how to plan and lay out a path, while Carol Klein celebrates the hardy geranium and the abundance of varieties that give masses of colour to the summer garden. Plus, the programme finds a Wolverhampton couple who have created a garden full of mystery and imagination.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond and Nick Nairn are in their own kitchens cooking with Scottish produce and Euan McIlwraith finds out how you can research the history of your own area using historic maps. Anne Lundon investigates how lockdown has impacted the National Trust for Scotland and the important work of the Air Ambulance.(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Brian Cunningham deal with viewers' homegrown questions. Carole identifies and deals with the garden Pest of the Week and Brian is back among his compost heap.(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Middle East
programme.desc: The writer visits Iran, starting in its bustling capital Tehran, where despite the boom in fast food outlets the dishes of the past still have a place in the hearts and culture of the people. Nigel is taken to lunch at his taxi driver's house to sample a popular stew known as dizi, then ventures north to the shores of the Caspian Sea to meet those who once fished for the famous beluga caviar, but now have to find other livings since sturgeon stocks have declined. Nigel explores the varied landscape, and harvests pomegranates and saffron, two items that epitomise Middle Eastern cuisine.(n)

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.sub-title: A Cook Abroad: Monica Galetti's France
programme.desc: The MasterChef: The Professionals judge embarks on a culinary road trip through the Jura region of eastern France, spending time with the area's food producers to see how their passion translates into world-class ingredients. She joins a boar hunt and faces up to the responsibility of eating meat, learns the secret of making some of the UK's favourite French cheeses and discovers an ancient profession she never knew existed. Finally, on the slopes of Mont d'Or, Monica prepares a rustic feast using produce she gathered herself.(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers continue their celebration of Britain's natural larder, focusing on the food provided by British waters as they fly-fish for trout and create a selection of dishes from the produce of seas and rivers.(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: The Bat Man of Mexico: Natural World
programme.desc: Since he first kept vampire bats in his bathroom as a child, Mexican biologist and ecologist Rodrigo Medellin has dedicated his life to saving all species of the flying mammal. In this film, David Attenborough narrates his quest to save the endangered lesser long-nosed bat, which is crucial to the production of tequila as it pollinates the plants the drink is made from. Medellin tracks the bats' epic migration across Mexico - braving hurricanes, snakes, Mayan tombs and seas of cockroaches.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Profiles of handsome heroes and pin-ups of the silver screen, including Paul Newman, Robert Redford, Jane Russell and Marilyn Monroe. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Guys and Dolls (1955)
programme.desc: A gambler accepts a bet that he cannot win the heart of a strait-laced Salvation Army member. Meanwhile, his friend has to find a secret location for an illegal dice game he is organising - while also contending with the police, a mobster and a girlfriend desperate to drag him to the altar. Musical, starring Frank Sinatra, Marlon Brando and Jean Simmons. Songs include Luck Be a Lady Tonight and Sit Down You're Rocking the Boat.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with the Heinz baked bean factory in Wigan, which has upped production to deliver almost 50 million cans in just a month, nearly double the number of tins the firm would normally sell in the same period. Operations manager John Brockley reveals how staff are keeping safe across the site, including passing through a thermal camera that automatically takes the temperature of workers as they enter the factory.(n)

programme.title: Turkey with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter explores the country that lies at the crossroads between Europe and Asia, and discovers the dramatic changes it is undergoing. The first leg of his journey begins in Istanbul, from which he travels along the Aegean coast to the hostile border with Syria. He meets refugees trying to start a new life and a billionaire taking advantage of a property boom. He moves on into the Taurus Mountains and the Black Sea Coast, and visits a community trying to keep an ancient language alive.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a selection of memorable moments where members of the panel competed with each other to secure investment in a promising entrepreneur's product. Pitches featured include an umbrella dispensing machine which won the show's first ever investment, a travel toy company that all five panellists wanted a share of, and a commercial submarine business.(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts an isolation version of his topical comedy show from his garage. A diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life - including Romesh's mother - are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today and the latest talking points in the media. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined remotely by a selection of celebrity guests, comedians and experts, to investigate how and why the events in the news matter to people across the country.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: Ally takes Dave with her when she attends her sister's wedding, where she is due to give the maid of honour speech. Meanwhile, Mike is trying to finalise Dave's first record deal on the very same weekend.(n)

programme.title: Dave
programme.desc: Having just signed his first record deal, Dave fights to push his own creative vision - in this case, a brand new and controversial track called Jail, which he has earmarked as his first ever release but which his record company strongly objects to.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: Angel's emotional entanglements distract him with lives on the line, while Emily quizzes both her husband and EZ's father on where their affections lie.(n)

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: A brutal abduction, decade-old accounts, and a corrupt clerk contribute to a hectic day for EZ Reyes as he strives to do right by his family and his gang.(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week.(n)

programme.title: Life and Birth
programme.desc: First-time mum Mini is induced three weeks early after experiencing reduced fetal movement, and with concerns growing it is ultimately decided to deliver the baby by caesarean section. Meanwhile, 19-year-old Billie-Joe has a rare twin pregnancy called TRAP Sequence, which means one of the twins hasn't developed properly and has no heartbeat. Supported by her boyfriend Josh, Billie-Joe undergoes a complex procedure at Birmingham Women's Hospital's Fetal Medicine Centre to save the life of their healthy baby.(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Guy Self returns to Holby, and is now in a relationship with a former patient, a development he wishes to keep a secret from a suspicious Ric. Essie begins her second round of chemotherapy, and her friends are determined to make sure that every other aspect of her life remain as normal as possible. Max makes an announcement regarding the future of Darwin, sending Jac and Kian's relationship into new territory.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Jay Blades' Home Fix
programme.desc: Joe Swift shows how to bring a lawn back to life, AJ Rose demonstrates how easy it is to put down decking, and Jay has a tip to help keep tools tidy.(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Anu and Ash live in Coventry with their cat Quinn, where their garden is currently a new build blank canvas divided into two cramped tiers. The couple want to turn it into a pet-friendly delight, inspired by the gardens and countryside they fell in love with on holidays in Tuscany, Italy. They want lots of interest for Quinn and a large inviting patio where they can entertain their friends and family. Arit Anderson and Charlie Dimmock have a £3,500 budget to make the couple's dreams come true.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift celebrate the positive power of plants as they focus on the designers and growers striving for a better world with the creations they bring to the event. They reveal how, over the years, the show has contributed to the better promotion of the health of the planet, plants and people, and talk to Countryfile presenter Ellie Harrison and BBC correspondent Fergal Keane about the importance of gardens to mental wellbeing.(n)

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver travels to Iona in the Inner Hebrides to learn about St Columba's tough brand of monasticism, and finds out how the epic journey of St Cuthbert led to the writing of the Lindisfarne Gospels and the building of Durham Cathedral. At Canterbury Cathedral, Neil discovers how the gruesome murder of Thomas Becket spurred the building of its nave, and on Glastonbury Tor in Somerset, he investigates the story of the Holy Grail, the sacred cup of everlasting life.(n)

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Chase the Case
programme.desc: Dan Walker hosts the strategic game show in which five contestants trade general knowledge for information to gain access to a case containing a secret amount of cash.(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen go on a culinary journey around the nation, following a route mapped out by the adventurous gourmand Samuel Chamberlain in his 1963 travelogue British Bouquet: An Epicurean Tour of Britain. They begin in the Dorset seaside town of Weymouth, where they sample lots of fresh seafood before heading to the countryside to eat Portland lamb.(n)

programme.title: Euros Rewind
programme.desc: Yugoslavia v Spain. A chance to see the Euro 2000 Group C encounter at Jan Breydel Stadium in Bruges, Belgium.(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman head to Bradford, where the Strictly Come Dancing judge has his first taste of a Pakistani curry and samples some Latvian cuisine.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Claire Rawle and James Lewis value antiques and collectibles at St Albans Cathedral and Abbey in Hertfordshire, while Paul Martin visits nearby Knebworth House.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble revisit dramatic events at Longleat. Kate visits the red panda maternity suite, Alfie the gorilla has surgery to address a recurring infection on his jaw, and the park goes into lockdown after reports of an escaped animal. Back in Animal Adventure, Jean Johansson checks in with keeper Kim and Orville the corn snake.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.sub-title: Back in Time for the Weekend
programme.desc: The Ashby-Hawkins family give up their 21st century technology and travel back in time to discover the radical transformation of our leisure time since 1950. Guided by presenter Giles Coren and social historian Polly Russell, the family's experience is underpinned by The Family Expenditure Survey, a government study which ran from the 1950s through to 1999. In the first episode, dad Rob finds out that he's expected to be handy with a tool kit, while mum Steph comes to terms with all the washing, cooking and cleaning expected of a 1950s housewife. Meanwhile, 16-year-old Daisy learns ballroom dancing courtesy of Angela Rippon, and 12-year-old Seth discovers the great outdoors.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.sub-title: Inside the Factory: Keeping Britain Going
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace catches up with workers at the Walkers Crisps factory in Leicester, which he visited in 2016, finding out how they are coping during the coronavirus crisis. He speaks to manager Carl Lewis, who reveals that in order to keep up with demand they're making big changes to their output, switching away from single packets to a situation where 90 per cent of production is now the multipacks sold in supermarkets.(n)

programme.title: Inside Monaco: Playground of the Rich
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix narrates a documentary giving an insight into the principality on the French Riviera, with access to the royal palace as well as the super-rich and those who serve them. In the first edition, director Michael Waldman explores how this extraordinary country operates, meeting everyone from Prince Albert and his staff to wealthy widow Monika Bacardi, who takes him on a tour in her chauffeur-driven classic Rolls-Royce. He also goes track side with ex-Formula One driver David Coulthard and team owner Eddie Jordan during the Monaco Grand Prix.(n)

programme.title: Comedians: Home Alone
programme.desc: Vic Reeves does his inimitable impersonation of Graham Norton, Sara Pascoe teaches yoga with her rescue dog, Reginald D Hunter gives his unique insight into conspiracy theories and Matt Berry showcases his exclusive anti-virus song. Also featuring Bob Mortimer, Tony Way, Adam Buxton and some very relaxed cats.(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as James Acaster, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Darren Harriott and Zoe Lyons join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look at the news. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga traces the house's fortunes in the 18th and 19th centuries. He begins by investigating the death of owner Joseph Holbrook, and discovering why his daughter was written out of the will. A later resident of the home rises to become mayor in the 1830s, but had to find a way to prevent riots that had ravaged the city years earlier from occurring again. David also delves into the life of a servant who worked in the house in the 1870s, only to leave domestic servitude for an abusive marriage, and discovers how a successful teacher who later owned the house ended up committed to an asylum.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Argyll, George and Sybil set off to gather in their sheep from over six square miles of rugged mountainside before taking some of them to market, while soon-to-be-wed couple Martin and Mel introduce their newly purchased rams to their flock of ewes. In the far north beyond Inverness, John heads to market to teach his 13-year-old son James how to buy a good flock of ewes, and Sandy sets off on a 1,600-mile journey from his home on Lewis to sell his specialist Hebridean mutton and beef around the UK.(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: The first of three programmes in which cameras explore some of the most inhospitable places on the planet and the people who live there, including a team of Sherpa roping a route on Mount Everest's notorious Khumbu Icefall and, on the slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro, farmers fight pitched battles to save their crops from marauding elephants. In the Amazon rainforest, two boys undergo a rite of passage involving hundreds of ants with the most painful stings on Earth, and conservationists in the Grand Canyon try to ensure the survival of one of America's few surviving condor chicks.(n)

programme.title: Billy Elliot (2000)
programme.desc: A northern youngster is pushed into taking boxing lessons by his macho dad - but secretly enrols in a ballet class instead, where it soon becomes clear he has a real talent for fancy footwork. However, he faces a tough time convincing his father that dance is a worthwhile pursuit. Comedy drama, starring Jamie Bell, Julie Walters and Gary Lewis.(n)

programme.title: The Bermuda Triangle: Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: Bruce Denardo explores the legend of the region where many planes and ships have disappeared in mysterious circumstances. Using a state-of-the-art submarine, Graham Hawkes reveals how five wrecks ended up 730ft down in the heart of the deadly expanse of water.(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Gagging for It - TV's Hunger for Radio Comedy
programme.desc: The history of radio comedies that have made the move to TV, from Hancock's Half Hour to Little Britain and Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy to Dead Ringers, including an insight into the golden rules for a successful transfer. With contributions by Barry Cryer, Jon Culshaw, Clive Anderson and Marcus Brigstocke. Part of Radio Week.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Sybil Ruscoe and Jenny Powell present the edition first shown on September 28, featuring performances by Wet Wet Wet, the Beautiful South, Gloria Estefan, Sydney Youngblood, Kate Bush, Erasure, Black Box and Karyn White.(n)

programme.title: John Williams at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances featuring the guitarist, from a BBC archive spanning 15 years. The programme includes classical masterworks, the prog rock of Sky and comedy with Eric Sykes. There are also duets with fellow guitar maestro Julian Bream.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: The first of three programmes in which cameras explore some of the most inhospitable places on the planet and the people who live there, including a team of Sherpa roping a route on Mount Everest's notorious Khumbu Icefall and, on the slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro, farmers fight pitched battles to save their crops from marauding elephants. In the Amazon rainforest, two boys undergo a rite of passage involving hundreds of ants with the most painful stings on Earth, and conservationists in the Grand Canyon try to ensure the survival of one of America's few surviving condor chicks.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Horizon: Oceans of the Solar System
programme.desc: Although oceans define the earth and are crucial to life, recent discoveries have revealed that they are not unique to earth. Oceans have been located across the solar system, and scientists are now embarking on a journey in search of new life in places that never seemed possible. With Nasa planning to dive to the depths of a strange, distant ocean in a remarkable submarine, the programme discovers that the hunt for oceans in space is marking the dawn of a new era in the search for alien life.(n)

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: For the birds and animals of the Highlands, the summer offers a chance to raise offspring before the days shorten and the storms of autumn arrive. Seabirds flock to the island of Handa off the Sutherland Coast, where 30,000 pairs of guillemots breed annually. Handa is also the location for a spectacular wild drama ending with the tiny chicks, known as jumplings, leaping for their lives from the 400-ft cliffs. Cameras also reveal life in the great rolling expanse of wetland known as the Flow Country, whose blanket peat bog stores more carbon than all of the forests in the entire UK.(n)

programme.title: Romeo and Juliet: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: Erica Whyman directs the 2018 RSC production of Shakespeare's most famous story of love at first sight. Starring Bally Gill and Karen Fishwick in the title roles.(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Arena: All The World's A Screen - Shakespeare on Film
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the history of films based on Shakespeare plays, from the silent era to the modern day, and featuring movies by directors as varied as Orson Welles, Akira Kurosawa, Franco Zeffirelli, Roman Polanski, Baz Luhrmann and Kenneth Branagh. The programme reveals how Laurence Olivier radically altered the performance of Shakespeare on film by directing and starring in Henry V and Hamlet, and explores how international film-makers from Russia, Japan, India and many other nations have tackled the material.(n)

programme.title: British Masters
programme.sub-title: We Are Making a New World
programme.desc: Art historian James Fox presents a celebration of 20th-century British painting, beginning with pieces by artists who worked before and during the First World War. He explores how painters Walter Sickert, Wyndham Lewis and David Bomberg fought against the traditions of 19th-century art, and analyses depictions of soldiers' lives - and deaths - by Christopher Nevinson, Paul Nash and Stanley Spencer.(n)

programme.title: Highlands: Scotland's Wild Heart
programme.desc: For the birds and animals of the Highlands, the summer offers a chance to raise offspring before the days shorten and the storms of autumn arrive. Seabirds flock to the island of Handa off the Sutherland Coast, where 30,000 pairs of guillemots breed annually. Handa is also the location for a spectacular wild drama ending with the tiny chicks, known as jumplings, leaping for their lives from the 400-ft cliffs. Cameras also reveal life in the great rolling expanse of wetland known as the Flow Country, whose blanket peat bog stores more carbon than all of the forests in the entire UK.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Horizon: Oceans of the Solar System
programme.desc: Although oceans define the earth and are crucial to life, recent discoveries have revealed that they are not unique to earth. Oceans have been located across the solar system, and scientists are now embarking on a journey in search of new life in places that never seemed possible. With Nasa planning to dive to the depths of a strange, distant ocean in a remarkable submarine, the programme discovers that the hunt for oceans in space is marking the dawn of a new era in the search for alien life.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross create a peaceful, dewy-brown mountain scene featuring a fresh morning lake and succulent evergreens.(n)

programme.title: Handmade in the Pacific
programme.desc: An insight into the creative processes and cultural significance of traditional artefacts from the Pacific region, beginning with making of an Aboriginal didgeridoo.(n)

programme.title: Andrew Marr's History of Modern Britain
programme.desc: The journalist examines the post-Thatcher years, considering the nation's final shift from an imperial power to an island at the heart of a strong world economy. He also recalls noteworthy developments under the leadership of John Major and Tony Blair, including Black Wednesday and the increasing awareness of global warming.(n)

programme.title: Ocean Autopsy: The Secret Story of Our Seas
programme.desc: Oceanographer Helen Czerski and zoologist George McGavin examine the health of the North Sea's ecosytem to reveal the startling changes the world's oceans are undergoing. They investigate the effects of high levels of life-threatening toxins on marine life, and the invisible plague of micro and nano plastics saturating the water, as well as a species of fungi that can potentially consume these microplastics. George also visits Utrecht University to see leading animal pathologist Lonneke Ijsseldijk perform a necropsy on a harbour porpoise to try to find out how and why it died.(n)

programme.title: Ocean Giants
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the lives of the planet's largest marine mammals and the secrets of their survival. Cameramen Doug Allan and Didier Noirot travel from the Indian Ocean to the Arctic seas, filming 200-ton blue whales and fighting humpbacks. The team also discovers that southern right whales possess a pair of one-ton testicles and reveals how Arctic bowheads can live to be more than 200 years old. Narrated by Stephen Fry.(n)

programme.title: Seven Ages of Britain
programme.desc: David Dimbleby tells the story of the power struggle between the monarchy and the Church during the Middle Ages through treasures of the period, and reveals how the code of chivalry brought the two sides together. He also reassesses the cultural impact of Richard II's reign, and examines artefacts including the Mappa Mundi, Chaucer's Canterbury Tales and Britain's only preserved medieval crown.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates go on an adventure to find the most unusual and amazing places in Asia. They explore the Keralan Backwaters on a boat made out of coconuts in India; track down the smell of Christmas at a cinnamon plantation in Sri Lanka; find out what's for sale at a floating market in Thailand; meet a man who makes robots out of rubbish in China and visit a Kanto Lantern festival in Japan, where they must keep giant bamboo poles balanced to win the Main Event trophy! Ed is joined on this bonkers road trip by Naomi Wilkinson, Inel Tomlinson, Lauren Layfield, Michelle Ackerley and Johny Pitts - there's stuff to do in Asia that is totally ace and it turns up All Over The Place.(n)

Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died.(n)

programme.desc: After living in the UK for 50 years, Anthony Bryan is called in by his manager at the care home where he works and told she has received a letter from the Home Office claiming that he has no right to work in the UK and is suspending him. A few months later, immigration officers arrive at Anthony's front door and he is put into a van and taken to a detention centre in Dorset. Drama inspired by a story from the Windrush scandal, starring Patrick Robinson, Nadine Marshall and Pippa Bennett-Warner.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Scandal at the Post Office - Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Up-and-coming writer Arabella returns from visiting her boyfriend in Italy and is greeted by a deluge of frantic messages from her literary agents. With a matter of hours left to finish a draft of her second book, she plans to pull an all-nighter - but is soon tempted to join a friend on a night out. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, created by and starring Michaela Coel, with Weruche Opia and Paapa Essiedu.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section.(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Steve Brown explores Elmley Nature Reserve on the Isle of Sheppey, where swallows and swifts are gathering in their hordes, heralding the arrival of summer. programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team of antiques experts visit Erddig, north Wales. On a scorching hot day, the crowds come out, bringing with them items including a dazzling emerald and diamond ring worn by the owner's grandmother on the Titanic, a Welsh love spoon carved in 1859 by a young man for his sweetheart and an avid fan's collection of James Bond props.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by four more sporting stars for the quiz hosted by Sue Barker. The panellist are England cricketer Rory Burns, who made his international debut in November 2018, British athlete Emily Diamond, who was part of the bronze medal-winning 4x400m team at Rio 2016, former swimmer Mark Foster, who competed primarily in butterfly and freestyle at 50 metres and won a gold medal at the World Short Course Championships, and former rugby league star Robbie Hunter-Paul, who played for four English clubs during a 19-year career.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones presents the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Scandal at the Post Office - Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian arranges a memorial for Dennis and Sharon is touched by the effort he has put in. Gray finds Whitney disorientated on the street and takes her back to his place, where they go over the night Leo was killed. Mick and Linda have their eyes on a house to rent, but they are worried about Phil paying them with dodgy money for The Vic. Peter asks Dotty to fix his car but she refuses.(n)

programme.title: Sitting In Limbo
programme.desc: After living in the UK for 50 years, Anthony Bryan is called in by his manager at the care home where he works and told she has received a letter from the Home Office claiming that he has no right to work in the UK and is suspending him. A few months later, immigration officers arrive at Anthony's front door and he is put into a van and taken to a detention centre in Dorset. Drama inspired by a story from the Windrush scandal, starring Patrick Robinson, Nadine Marshall and Pippa Bennett-Warner.(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various animals go undercover in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. Spy Pup is immersed in wild dog life to explore their maternal love, and Spy Penguin records the turbulent action of the mating season, as a male Adelie penguin falls victim to a pebble thief that ruins his chances of love for the season. Spy Tortoise and Spy Bushbaby keep watch on chimpanzees, while in perhaps the most moving scenes, Spy Monkey becomes the focus of attention for a troop of concerned langurs who believe the motionless camera-creature has died.(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Panorama
programme.sub-title: Scandal at the Post Office - Panorama
programme.desc: Hundreds of sub-postmasters were jailed or financially ruined after computers said money was missing from their branches, but the Post Office has admitted that its Horizon system can make mistakes. But when did senior managers find this out, and did they continue to prosecute postmasters for stealing when they knew technology could be to blame? Reporter Nick Wallis investigates what could be Britain's biggest-ever miscarriage of justice scandal and uncovers evidence of a cover-up at the Post Office.(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ian arranges a memorial for Dennis and Sharon is touched by the effort he has put in. Gray finds Whitney disorientated on the street and takes her back to his place, where they go over the night Leo was killed. Mick and Linda have their eyes on a house to rent, but they are worried about Phil paying them with dodgy money for The Vic. Peter asks Dotty to fix his car but she refuses.(n)

programme.title: Sitting In Limbo
programme.desc: After living in the UK for 50 years, Anthony Bryan is called in by his manager at the care home where he works and told she has received a letter from the Home Office claiming that he has no right to work in the UK and is suspending him. A few months later, immigration officers arrive at Anthony's front door and he is put into a van and taken to a detention centre in Dorset. Drama inspired by a story from the Windrush scandal, starring Patrick Robinson, Nadine Marshall and Pippa Bennett-Warner.(n)

programme.title: Pretty Single
programme.desc: Part-time model and personal trainer Ali is looking for love with someone who will make her laugh, but finds it tough preparing for a series of dates without make-up or fancy clothes.(n)

programme.title: I May Destroy You
programme.desc: Up-and-coming writer Arabella returns from visiting her boyfriend in Italy and is greeted by a deluge of frantic messages from her literary agents. With a matter of hours left to finish a draft of her second book, she plans to pull an all-nighter - but is soon tempted to join a friend on a night out. Drama exploring the question of sexual consent in contemporary life, created by and starring Michaela Coel, with Weruche Opia and Paapa Essiedu.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a new variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section.(n)

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In some countries the coronavirus pandemic appears to have enhanced national unity and solidarity, in others it's exposed deep fault lines. In India the crisis has hit the poorest migrant workers disproportionately hard; it's also deepened tensions between the Hindu majority and Muslim minority. Is that the fault of the Hindu nationalist BJP government? HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to General Secretary of the BJP Ram Madhav. Is Prime Minister Modi stoking communal tensions even in a national emergency?(n)

programme.desc: The Health and Social Care select committee session on the management of the coronavirus outbreak with evidence from Professor Christophe Fraser of the University of Oxford, former Taiwanese vice-president Chen Chien-jen, Baroness Harding, the executive chair of the NHS's Test and Trace programme and Lord Deighton, adviser to the Health Secretary on personal protective equipment, from Wednesday 3 June.(n)

programme.title: Superstore

programme.title: Dress to Impress

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The host talks to actor James Marsden about the return of sitcom Dead to Me, and chats with Alanis Morissette, who performs her latest single, Smiling.(n)

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Tucker from the East Midlands is hoping to be impressed by the style choices made for him by three single women.(n)

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness lines up another panel of single ladies, giving them a chance to appraise the worthiness of a selection of bachelors seeking romance. Among the men entering the 'love lift' this week are fitness instructor Dathan from London, pianist Scott from Huddersfield, research executive George from Surrey, and Nick, a construction worker from New Zealand.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedy from Harry Hill, inspired by viewers' home videos, featuring a baby doing a spot of fracking, toddlers wrestling for a place in a good nursery, and the fun that can be had with a squashed Womble. Plus, another Video You'll Wish You'd Never Seen.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers, featuring home-made bombs, an emu stealing a smartphone, and tips on how not to trim a tree. Plus, Holly Willoughby and Phillip Schofield lookalikes in an amusing situation.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: A selection of home videos sent in by members of the public and presented in a format to entertain people of all ages, narrated by Harry Hill. A hot air balloon demolishes a church, a baby hits the bottle and one unlucky child ends up on the sacrificial altar.(n)

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A squirrel steals a mobile phone, a fox attacks a golfer and a beach wedding is ruined. Plus, a skunk stinks out a swimming pool and a toddler murders a smurf.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden and Alan consider adopting a six-year-old boy called Louis after Ms McMartin makes the suggestion, and they try to make him feel at home - but they may not get on as well as hoped.(n)

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Alan and Walden officially become foster parents to Louis, who has experience wrapping caregivers around his little finger. Alan tries to be the 'cool' parent, leaving Walden to be the strict voice of reason and their different styles of parenting causes strain to their friendship. Starring Jon Cryer and Ashton Kutcher.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: 8 Simple Rules for Buying My Teenage Daughter
programme.desc: Peter runs up a massive bill at the pharmacy and decides to sell Meg to the Goldmans to help pay it off - much to their son Neil's delight. However, she becomes jealous when he starts dating someone else. Meanwhile, Stewie falls in love with his new babysitter.(n)

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.sub-title: Breaking Out Is Hard to Do
programme.desc: Lois is sent to prison for persistent shoplifting, plunging the family into such disarray that Peter feels obliged to help her escape. Forced to go on the run, they take refuge in 'Asian Town' - where Joe eventually tracks them down.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan prays for a friend and is delighted to have his request fulfilled - until he learns his new pal is an atheist, at which point he sets out to prove that God exists by making his acquaintance's life extremely hard. Meanwhile, Roger convinces Steve that he has been accepted into Hogwarts.(n)

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine's adoptive parents visit for an unannounced holiday with the family, and Stan becomes angry when they refuse to fall in with his 'man of the house' attitude. Steve tries to impress a girl at school by living to the extreme.(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper plans to convert the flats into smaller dwelling units - but he has not bargained for Robin, Chrissy and Jo having a three-year lease. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Nurse Maggie Simkiss cares for end of life patients and treats their emotional well-being as well as their direct medical needs. To say thank you for her hard work, Alan Titchmarsh and his team of green-fingered experts arrive at her Hull home and help transform the unkempt garden into a grown-up Mediterranean hideaway.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team come to the aid of nonagenarian Roberta and her 86-year-old best friend and carer Enid, who live together in a retirement bungalow in Wiveliscombe, Somerset. Originally from Liverpool, the two plucky pensioners have helped each other out countless times throughout the course of their friendship, which has lasted seven decades. However, they now find themselves trapped in their home by a treacherous slope in their garden, so Alan plans to set them free by designing a future-proofed garden in a vintage style.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff gains entry to Number 6 to delete the CCTV footage, Roy finds Shona lurking outside the café, and Dev decides to move the twins to Oakhill School.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya makes a shocking discovery regarding Geoff, Asha's problems follow her to her new school and Roy invites Shona to move into his flat.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff is forced to come clean about hiring escorts, Asha finds a renewed confidence and Shona agrees to live with Roy rather than David.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy set out to find a motive for a terminally ill property developer's murder. Many of the Badger's Drift residents have reason to hold a grudge against their charmless neighbour, including a theatre director with unhappy childhood memories, whom Cully gets the chance to work with. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Phyllis Logan, and featuring Toby Jones' first appearance in a recurring role as pathologist Dan Peterson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Members of the Midsomer-in-the-Marsh Ornithological Society fall out when Ralph Ford claims to have spotted a blue-crested hoopoe, meaning he wins the group's year-listing competition. However, their president Patrick Morgan says the rare species could never have been seen so far away from its native Uganda and brands him a cheat. The next day, Barnaby and Jones have a murder case on their hands when Patrick is found dead at the scene of the reported sighting. Guest starring Paul Nicholls and James Dreyfus.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A well-known eccentric contacts the Belgian sleuth when he is haunted by suicidal nightmares. However, he fails to follow Poirot's advice, and is found dead soon afterwards. Drama, starring David Suchet, with Joely Richardson and Mary Tamm.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Du Plessis is haunted by his belief that Danny murdered their old enemy Christian Peeters, and the friendship between the two men is tested to its limit. Meanwhile, Caroline, Liv and Charlotte return, only to find their new home falling apart and a mysterious illness affecting the vultures and animals on the reserve. African-set wildlife drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Deon Stewardson and Hayley Mills.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie is left panicked when Andrea and Millie disappear, while Nate approaches Rhona with a proposition, suggesting they split half the restaurant order with Butlers. Brenda seeks an apology from Amelia for egging her window, but when Amelia maintains her innocence, Kerry tells Brenda to clear off.(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jamie panics when Tracy reveals that Andrea left without her purse, cards, money or car. Kerry is pained as she overhears an emotional Amelia confessing to God that she egged Brenda's windows.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A former bookie falls under suspicion for the murder of a hospital benefactor, who is found dead in his car. As the detectives investigate the case, it becomes clear their prime suspect had a strong motive, believing the victim was responsible for putting his daughter into a coma. Brian Cox guest stars, with John Thaw and Kevin Whately.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A village of Apern Tallow is rocked by a series of ghostly occurrences that begins with the inexplicable slashing of a painting in the local museum. Barnaby investigates, shadowed by Cully's actor boyfriend, who is preparing for a role as a police officer - but the mysterious events take a far darker turn when a man is found dead on the grave of his ancestor. Prunella Scales guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Follow That Camel (1967)
programme.desc: A disgraced English gentleman joins the Foreign Legion to restore his honour, and proves himself an unlikely hero. Meanwhile, his naive lost love learns he was innocent, and heads to the desert to bring him home. Comedy, starring Phil Silvers, Jim Dale, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey, Peter Butterworth, Bernard Bresslaw and Joan Sims.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the cloistered world of Midsomer Priory when a nun is found strangled. The detectives uncover a complex mystery involving holy orders, teenage romantic liaisons, African art and missing antique silver as the community is forced to open its doors to 21st-century policing. Former EastEnders actor Cliff Parisi (Minty) guest stars alongside Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted).(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Lisa tries to make amends for her part in Lucinda's disappearance but is starting to see a different side to Kate and Guy. Her husband Joe tells her that she should focus on her own family, as their daughter Sally has struggles to cope with the recent events. Guy learns that Sally has been keeping information from the investigation and storms round to Lisa's home. Meanwhile, Detective Constable Joanne Aspinall starts to look into the Rivertys' family history, and grows suspicious of Guy. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar.(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A homicidal rapist escapes from prison and embarks on a deadly game of cat and mouse with Morse, relying on his mastery of disguise and bizarre occult connections to stay one step ahead of the law. Only the madman's psychiatrist seems able to provide a lead as to his whereabouts - and her life is in mortal danger as a result. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Keith Allen, Richard Griffiths and Harriet Walter.(n)

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: The search for a serial killer in the area around Loch Ness becomes a matter of life and death for local detective Annie Redford, who is trying to cope with her first murder case. When the body of a local man is found at the foot of Carn Mohr Mountain and an isolated human heart on the loch shore, the town's normality is shattered and the nightmare begins. Thriller, starring Laura Fraser and Siobhan Finneran.(n)

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Danny finds a potential lifeline for the family as he pitches for a grant from a pharmaceutical company looking to fund an environmental project. The money would enable them to buy their former home, but Danny's old boss Ed Lynch turns out to be his rival. Du Plessis is determined to do whatever it takes to prevent the sale of Leopard's Den to Lynch - but his plan has disastrous consequences.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Aircraft manufacturer Lord Mayfield hosts a party to trap a woman suspected of being a spy for the Germans. Fearing events might not go according to plan, Lady Mayfield decides to summon the famous Belgian detective to keep an eye on proceedings. Starring David Suchet, with John Stride and Phyllida Law.(n)
