programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina's plan to have Zelda nominated for Professor of the Year goes awry when the committee discovers that she attended college in 1873 and has her fired from her job.(n)

programme.title: Sabrina the Teenage Witch
programme.desc: Sabrina decides to take matters into her own hands when she gets an eerie feeling that she is being followed, ending up face to face with her mother.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe puts a ban on the use of all mobiles and computers over the weekend in the hope of improving communication within the family. However, he soon comes to regret his decision when he is offered a new job and needs to get back to his prospective employer in time to accept the post.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Joe is furious when he finds out that Dani had some older friends over to the house who drank his expensive bottle of wine, but wonders whether she is being totally honest with him about the event. When Lennox expresses admiration for her college professor Evita, Mel grows jealous of their relationship, until Evita asks to interview Mel for her latest research project. Shalita Grant guest stars alongside Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Now that she knows the truth about Margot, Riley decides to look for her biological father too, and uses her parents' high-school yearbook as a guide. With a little help from Ben, she narrows down the possible candidates to an easy-going pastry chef and a high-flying lawyer. Comedy, starring Chelsea Kane and Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley looks forward to meeting Philip's sister when she visits from England, but her excitement vanishes when it becomes clear his sibling is unimpressed with his taste in girlfriends. Meanwhile, Danny's contract negotiations with the Rangers are put in jeopardy. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is hurt when Professor Proton (Bob Newhart) contacts Leonard for help, so he befriends TV scientist Bill Nye, and Raj gets jealous when Howard crashes a girls' night in making home-made jewellery. Starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki, and Kunal Nayyar.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard becomes concerned that Sheldon will overreact when his friend learns the truth about a past indiscretion, while Penny confronts Raj's old flame Lucy about the way she ended their relationship. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry makes fun of Adam for taking part in a school musical production, but when he finds out that Adam and Lainey have been cast in roles that require them to kiss, he tries to sabotage it by joining the stage crew. Meanwhile, substitute teacher Beverly causes chaos in Erica's class. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Adam creates a video that makes it look as though he and his friends beat Barry in a game of basketball, Barry demands a rematch in the school gym. Beverly tries to throw a 'surprise' party for Murray's 50th birthday, despite his objections to the idea.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta hits it off with Sophia, a woman he meets in a bar, only to find out she is the defence attorney representing a perpetrator he is trying to put behind bars. Diaz pressures Santiago to stand as a union rep, while Gina and Boyle try to move on from their relationship. Eva Longoria (best known as Desperate Housewives' Gaby) joins the cast.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The precinct goes into lockdown on Thanksgiving when a mysterious powder-filled package is delivered to the building. Peralta, who has been left in charge while Holt and Jeffords are out of the office, is confident he can keep morale high and avoid any chaos - but things soon spiral out of control.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Howard are furious when they learn that Sheldon went to work with the military behind their backs. Angered, the pair ask Kripke to help them get their own back on Sheldon, much to his annoyance. Meanwhile, Bernadette suspects that Ruchi might be trying to steal her job while she is on bed rest, so she asks Raj to investigate for her, putting him in a very awkward position. Dean Norris guest stars.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys find a laptop that has some virtual currency on it. The only snag is, the computer belongs to Penny's ex-boyfriend Zack. Meanwhile, a seven-year-old video reveals a secret about Leonard and Penny's relationship.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel launches a healthy diet campaign while running for a Senate seat, but a voter soon catches her out eating some unhealthy fast food. When the man tweets a photo of her gorging on a burger and calls her a hypocrite, she gets into a war of words with him.(n)

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel receives unexpected news that could have a direct impact on her attempt to run for a senate seat. Ryder receives his orders from the US Navy, and a car crash gives Lenin and Zander a new outlook on life. Starring Melissa Joan Hart and Joey Lawrence.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley doubts her future with Ben after a psychic reading suggests their relationship is doomed. Ben tries to convince her that they are soulmates, but realises he knows very little about what makes her happy. Tucker does his best to persuade Bonnie to move out of the flat, while Danny asks Bonnie's ex-boyfriend Brad to help him cover up the failure of his Paris adventure.(n)

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Danny saves the day when Ben and Riley are mugged - but cannot take any of the credit because he is still pretending that he lives in Paris. Tucker realises that such a great human interest story gives him the perfect opportunity to save his job on Mary Hart's TV show, so he invites Ben and Riley to appear on the programme to talk about their experience. Mary Hart guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Peralta notices Holt is in a bad mood, he teams up with the captain's husband Kevin to find out what has gone wrong. Boyle tries to get a confession out of an elderly bank-robbery suspect (played by Happy Days creator Garry Marshall), who pretends to be frail and adorable whenever Santiago and Diaz are around.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: As the wedding of Gina's mother and Boyle's father arrives, each member of the squad is given a task to complete for the big day. However, Peralta and Santiago get sidetracked chasing a criminal, and Jeffords struggles to officiate at the ceremony. Comedy, guest starring Sandra Bernhard and Nick Cannon.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry plans big romantic gestures for Lainey on high school prom night, while Erica and Geoff try to attend the event together, but things don't go as planned. Adam is thrilled when a nerdy but cute girl asks him out, but is horrified when Beverly gets involved.(n)

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry attempts to drive a wedge between Adam and Pops when they debate the merits of a recently released Batman movie. Erica deliberately makes a mess of her interview at one university so that she will only receive an offer from her first choice of college, but her plan goes badly awry. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey, Sean Giambrone and Hayley Orrantia.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny gets the opportunity to spend time with Bill Gates at work, because his business wants to partner with her pharmaceutical company. Leonard, Howard and Raj do everything in their power to also meet the high-tech entrepreneur, while Sheldon thinks he's the victim of an April Fool's Day prank.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard runs against Sheldon to be president of the tenants' association. Howard and Raj find a drone in the backyard.(n)

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester.(n)

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: The precinct's staff prepare for an unusually busy night as the neighbourhood gets into the Halloween spirit, and Amy falls into a particularly foul mood when she is forced to wear a fancy-dress costume during an undercover operation. Meanwhile, Jake bets Captain Holt that he can steal his Medal of Valour before midnight - a challenge that prompts him to don his own selection of disguises.(n)

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Carl plans a Spanish wedding for his fiancee Rebecca, as he tries to arrange the perfect day to meet her expectations for a high-class affair.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.sub-title: Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Ant Middleton gets his training staff to set the recruits a series of challenges designed to expose their weaknesses. An expedition up the side of a mountain quickly takes its toll, with one celebrity contestant collapsing and another contracting hypothermia in the extreme cold.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson hosts the show in which potential dates bare all bit by bit in a bid to prove they are the one. First up is 27-year-old flight attendant Alex from Bristol. Having never had a serious relationship, he is hoping to land himself a man for the long haul. He is followed by 26-year-old Laura from Gloucestershire, a horse-loving country girl who is looking for love.(n)

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: During a field trip to the zoo, Xander and four other students are taken over by the spirits of hyenas. The five possessed teenagers form a savage gang, and as Xander develops a cruel new personality and rejects Willow, his fellow pack members progress from bullying weaker students to killing and eating the principal.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Amber Moss and Colin Webster from Edinburgh's Portobello district to transform their seaside home into a 1960s-themed haven that reflects their personalities. She introduces her suggestion on cool concrete and gives the couple a lesson in how to create screen-print furniture.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: After years spent dedicated to their demanding careers in nursing and social work, Gillian and Paul Clarke from Stockport have decided it's time to start taking it a little easier and put themselves first. They've got their hearts set on a holiday home on Spain's Costa Cálida where they can boogie their way to retirement. Jonnie Irwin shows the couple five properties for their budget of £45,000.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: After becoming foster parents to three young children, Jo Danson and her husband Bill decided a holiday home would be the perfect way for everyone to spend quality time together. Working within a budget of £65,000, Jasmine Harman shows Jo five suitable properties in the Orihuela Costa region of Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Birmingham pair Derek Hansel and Gina Awoko are looking for a home in Almeria, Spain, that they can eventually retire to and also generate some rental income from. Jasmine Harman shows them a range of properties within their £300,000 budget, from a coastal renovation project to an inland cortijo. However, it remains to be seen whether she can deliver the perfect property in the perfect location.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in Kent as they help two more couples on their property search. Kirstie is with Savannah and John, who are desperate to find their first home to buy in Maidstone, while Phil is with Londoners Matt and Tricia, who are looking for a home in the Kent countryside on a budget of £1.5million.(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen host the second round of the culinary challenge featuring teams of professional pastry chefs from top hotels, restaurants and small businesses. This week, the five remaining pairs are challenged to create millefeuille that look exactly like eggs and a bird-themed montee which must be adorned with enough Paris-Brest to serve 24. Judges Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden are on hand to assess the results of their efforts and decide which team will be leaving the contest this week.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Fun-loving Sonya Smith is the first to host the dinner-party challenge from Reading, Berkshire, and aims to win over the guests with a Touch of Sparkle theme. Aware her cooking skills leave a lot to be desired, she hopes to bungle her way through the night, and though she is amused by the kitchen chaos, not everyone is impressed.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Londoner Glen Wilkie hosts the second dinner party of the week in Reading, Berkshire, and is confident of victory. However, a disaster with his dessert gets the day off to a bad start, and his main course, which he has chosen to call Bambi on a Plate, leaves one guest in tears - while another flatly refuses to eat it.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mature student Richard Howse hosts the third dinner party in Reading, using a menu and set of instructions provided by his girlfriend to guide him. A treasure hunt brings out Glen Wilkie's feelings about Serine Daou, Sonya Smith dislikes some of the gluten-free food and, for the third night in a row, Sabi Phagura is served a plate of meat she will not eat.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth dinner party in Reading sees fitness fanatic Sabi Phagura putting her healthy regimen to one side to host a bhangra-themed evening. Unfortunately, neither the traditional Indian food on the table nor the entertainment seems to impress the guests.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Corset-maker Serine Daou hosts the final night of the culinary competition in Reading, Berkshire, and hopes to impress with her burlesque-themed party. When the gourmet burgers she serves fail to impress, she must rely on the evening's entertainment to put her back on course for the £1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.sub-title: The King's Head and Queen's Jules
programme.desc: Tim and Jules Anderson are first to host at the King's Head and Queen's Jules in Suffolk, where they provide their guests with a lesson in how to fillet fish, and an evening of karaoke. However, the owners find criticism hard to take the following morning.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gordon and Blossom welcome their rivals to the Hotel Continental in Harwich, Essex, where they are greeted by an erotic art display. The guests enjoy participating in a poetry reading during the afternoon, each writing their own piece, but the morning's breakfast goes awry after the hosts have a disagreement.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ryan Davies has to deal with a crisis when the contestants stay his five-star B&B Llanerch Vineyard in south Wales, but still manages to impress with his location and selection of wines. However, Gordon and Blossom go on a fault-finding mission and leave feedback the host is not happy with.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Neil and Tracy Bullows, owners of the Whitehall Hotel in Darwen, Lancashire, play host to the last night of the week's competition. Hoping to impress with a mix of professionalism and efficiency, they decide to take their guests on a tour of a local paint factory, but tempers fray and a stony silence follows at breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table.(n)

programme.title: Princess Diana's 'Wicked' Stepmother
programme.desc: An examination of the turbulent relationship between Diana, Princess of Wales and her formidable stepmother Raine Spencer, who rose from humble origins with the help of her larger-than-life mother, the novelist Barbara Cartland, to become a countess and a member of the royal family. Raine was a divisive figure, who was doted on by her husband Johnnie Spencer but loathed by Diana and her siblings. Yet as the princess's marriage collapsed, she reached out to her stepmother for support, marking a change in the women's relationship.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 18-year-old is rushed in after breaking his leg while playing football, and doctors worry it may have long-term implications. Meanwhile, David, who is 29, is airlifted to St George's following a head-on collision at speed, and 94-year-old Mary arrives after falling backwards down 14 steps and losing consciousness.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Billy is rushed to St George's after being crushed by falling bricks that were being stored on his front drive, while six-year-old Luther has a metal washer stuck on his finger. Senior healthcare play specialist Tracy talks about her job, which is to comfort and distract children during their treatment in A&E, and 83-year-old Audrey arrives with her grandson Lee after damaging her wrist while falling on holiday.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The first episode of the hit comedy. The Craggy Island cleric dreams of achieving stardom on the Faith of Our Fathers TV show - but his hopes are dashed by Jack and Dougal's untimely intervention. Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly star.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A sense of dread descends on Craggy Island as the priests prepare for poker-faced Father Stone's annual visit - and Ted tries desperately to think of a scheme to get rid of him. Clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan taking on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Katherine Ryan. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley is in charge of the letters and numbers.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An 18-year-old is rushed in after breaking his leg while playing football, and doctors worry it may have long-term implications. Meanwhile, David, who is 29, is airlifted to St George's following a head-on collision at speed, and 94-year-old Mary arrives after falling backwards down 14 steps and losing consciousness.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Eight-year-old Billy is rushed to St George's after being crushed by falling bricks that were being stored on his front drive, while six-year-old Luther has a metal washer stuck on his finger. Senior healthcare play specialist Tracy talks about her job, which is to comfort and distract children during their treatment in A&E, and 83-year-old Audrey arrives with her grandson Lee after damaging her wrist while falling on holiday.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt visits the town of Tequila on the trail of Mexico's national drink as he tries to find out where the idea of a worm in the bottle came from, and Jimmy Doherty learns how Poles transform carp into a much-loved national delicacy. Kate Quilton visits Spain to explore the difference between tangerines, mandarins, satsumas and clementines.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore travel to Worcestershire to pick up classic car fan Lee's 1965 VW Karmann Ghia, a once-stylish German motor that has now passed its sell-by date. Before they can return the restored vehicle to its surprised owner, the team must get to work replacing the starter motor and repairing the floorpan welding, accident-damaged body work and leaking engine.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend secretly fix up an Italian-car enthusiast's rusty 1994 Lancia Delta Integrale Evo II, but tragedy strikes and the job takes on a very different meaning.(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend team up with former Monte Carlo Rally winner Paddy Hopkirk to fix up a 1989 Peugeot 205 GTi, the first rally car they have ever worked on.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Documentary following Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree through the process of buying and restoring a French chateau that has been empty for the past four decades. Their new home has 12 acres of land, nine outbuildings, a walled garden and a moat - but it also has no electricity, heating or water, and proves to be a far more complicated DIY project than expected.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.sub-title: Chickens, Heating and a Blank Canvas
programme.desc: Dick and Angel decide they will live as self-sufficiently as they can while restoring the chateau, and invest in some chickens to provide them with eggs for breakfast every morning. However, they still have countless problems to deal with, and have only £20,000 left to spend on the project. The chateau is still without either heating or water, while the 59 windows are crumbling. While Dick's renovations fall behind schedule, Angel is in her element coming up with ideas for what the finished building will look like.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of the African-themed Foxholm B&B in North Yorkshire begins another competition, trying to prove their establishment offers the best value for money. Julie Janes and Yvonne Quinsey initially impress their guests with a cupcake class, but tempers fray when John Shields makes an unfavourable remark about German-born Sigrid.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wendy Johnson and John Shields welcome their rival hoteliers to the Pack Horse, an American diner-themed B&B in Lincolnshire, for the second round of the competition. A horse-riding session turns sour when Steve Reed takes a dislike to Nick Lawler, and matters get worse over dinner - before a pancake shortage at breakfast causes problems for the hosts.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Steve and Sigrid Reed host at their Glenwalden B&B in Blackpool. They hope ballroom dancing will entertain the guests, some of whom object to the pink flock wallpaper and knitted toilet roll holders. Nick Lawler seems to be warming to the owners, but one of them suspects his change of heart is all part of a cunning plan.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nick Lawler and business partner Kate Wilson host the competition at the Winder Hall Country House B&B in the Lake District, but a clash of egos ensues when they try to lecture the other participants about how to manage their hospitality establishments, and a disastrous breakfast service leaves contestants unimpressed.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Make-up artist and female impersonator Marcus Hunter-Neill hopes to impress with a red carpet-themed evening as he hosts the first party in Belfast. However, his alter ego Lady Portia Diamante steals the limelight as he competes for the £1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jamaican lifeguard Lenny Irving serves up Caribbean cuisine at the second of the week's dinner parties in Belfast. He also hopes to win over his guests by complementing his menu with strong rum - and seems particularly keen to impress Merchant Navy officer Fiona Rush.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Freelance feminist writer Cat Anderson hosts the third dinner party in Belfast, and hopes to win over her guests with her bohemian chic style and live music. However, her efforts are wasted on lifeguard Lenny Irving, who is only interested in Fiona Rush.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth dinner-party challenge from Belfast is hosted by charity worker and confident cook Rosie Smyth. She decides the best way to win over her guests is to have a 'come-as-your-teenage-self' evening, and a zingy mojito cheesecake.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Merchant Navy officer Fiona Rush hosts the final dinner party in Belfast, and hopes to impress with a nautical-themed evening. However, one of her guests is unimpressed with the amount of fish on the menu, before the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud revisits Denise and Bruno Del Tufo in Ashford, Kent, who hoped to transform a concrete water tower designed by Edwin Lutyens into a contemporary home. Kevin was originally far from enthusiastic about their project, believing the original building to be ugly and brutal in its design - but the couple soon had other problems to worry about, as rising steel prices forced them to make drastic changes to their budget.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A recovering alcoholic rings for help with her withdrawal symptoms, and a 10-year-old girl suffering from chest pains is discovered to be taking anti-psychotic medication.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 58-year-old woman is brought into the hospital after being run over by a lorry, suffering severe blood loss. Doctors are concerned that nerves and tissue in her leg may have suffered irreparable damage. Meanwhile, a 65-year-old man is treated after falling from a loft ladder, and a mischievous 85-year-old shows signs of severe dehydration.(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on Wiltshire's police, paramedics and fire services. Cases include a man with an axe-related injury, while another team respond to a Saturday afternoon call-out to help the victim of an injury incurred while playing rugby. And at a road traffic incident, a woman has to be cut out of her car and faces life-changing multiple injuries.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In the conclusion of Gordon Ramsay's visit to La Galleria 33 in Boston, Massachusetts, he confronts sisters Rita and Lisa with an ultimatum, and finds himself stuck in a war between the waiters, chefs and owners over who is to blame for the Italian restaurant's failings.(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: A recovering alcoholic rings for help with her withdrawal symptoms, and a 10-year-old girl suffering from chest pains is discovered to be taking anti-psychotic medication.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

Doctors are concerned that nerves and tissue in her leg may have suffered irreparable damage. Meanwhile, a 65-year-old man is treated after falling from a loft ladder, and a mischievous 85-year-old shows signs of severe dehydration.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A sense of dread descends on Craggy Island as the priests prepare for poker-faced Father Stone's annual visit - and Ted tries desperately to think of a scheme to get rid of him. Clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates shock reports that changing sea temperatures mean squid and chips could one day replace fish and chips as the national dish. Kate Quilton looks into where multivitamins come from and how much we need them, while Matt Tebbutt visits the UK's leading clotted cream producer in Cornwall and an astonishing algae farm in the desert in Israel.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Having sold a successful business, Ruth and Philip Trinder from Northamptonshire want to buy an apartment on the Balearic island of Majorca, where they can spend half the year. They want somewhere they can entertain family and friends, within easy walking distance of one of the island's stylish ports. With a budget of £300,000 Laura Hamilton sets out to help them find something with the contemporary look they love.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Leeds and Preston in search of people's junk and clutter to upcycle into valuable new items. Henry makes an international set of clocks out of dashboard time pieces, while Simon builds an eccentric chandelier from car headlights.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Cheshire and the Wirral, where items include a 1920s dance hall lighting system, an antique butter churn and an old enamel advertising sign.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Arthur race to collect low-hanging fruit to make delicious redcurrant jelly. In south-west France, Donna and Paul are hard at work on their most ambitious project yet, creating a tea room. However, stringent building regulations add to the challenge. Elsewhere, Ithaca and Alex need to raise some cash to help repair their chateau's 300-year-old roof.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: A couple in Essex with five daughters plan to build a contemporary home in a woodland clearing, but the budgets and schedules soon get out of control.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie welcome their guests to the Watersedge Guest House in Barton-on-Sea, Hampshire, but an afternoon of deer-watching leaves some of them bored. Desperate to impress their fellow B&B owners, the pair host a Hawaiian barbecue, before pulling out all the stops at breakfast time.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Owners Tim and Amelia Whitaker welcome their guests to their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast, and take them out on a boat for a spot of seal watching and diving. However, the sight of wet suits proves too much for one contestant, and tensions also flare over dinner when Mike and Danny are quizzed about their relationship.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eva Buschmann hosts the competition at the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, Dorset. She makes the mistake of assuming Danny Robinson and Mike Gibson are a couple, and offers them a double room with an exposed shower, but hopes a lesson in abstract art and a meal at her favourite harbour-side restaurant will win the other contestants over.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mike Gibson and Danny Robinson host their rival B&B owners at their four-star guesthouse - Homecliffe Hotel in Blackpool. The hosts take their visitors on a tram ride along the seafront and on to Madame Tussauds, but there are complaints about uncomfortable mattresses and disappointing views and the next day's breakfast fails to impress.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to discover how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers and air their grievances. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie start the proceedings, but confront the guests who complained about their room having no en-suite facilities, before the winner is announced.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien repurposes an old butcher's block into a coffee table, while Henry Cole restores a pair of go-karts to race against restorer Guy.(n)

programme.title: Countdown

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

programme.title: Grand Designs

The couple employed a method of building that allowed them to assemble the property in just 15 days on a budget of £300,000, but with so much attention given to the speed of construction, there was little time to think how the house might be to live in.(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: Norman Conquest of Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to the south-west corner of Wales and one of Britain's finest coastal paths to find out why Pembrokeshire feels so English, even though it's more than 100 miles from the border. With its numerous castles and a fine cathedral, there are sure signs the Normans stamped their mark over the area. Tony's four-day walk traces the story of their conquest of the county, which he discovers was very different from the knockout victory at Hastings, involving a long, fiercely fought struggle.(n)

programme.title: History's Greatest Hoaxes
programme.desc: The stories of famous hoaxes, featuring personal accounts of those who perpetrated and exposed them, beginning with the infamous alien autopsy footage.(n)

programme.title: Duran Duran: There's Something You Should Know
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, John Taylor, Nick Rhodes and Roger Taylor open up about the highs and lows of the band's career - with archive footage of former guitarist Andy Taylor helping round out the story. The documentary charts their trajectory through seven of their 14 albums, each one uncovering a compelling chapter in the history of five lads from Birmingham who had one simple dream - to become the biggest band in the world. Contributors include Boy George, Nile Rodgers and Mark Ronson.(n)

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek, with help from the Mathewson clan, drag a mini pickup out of the barn where it's been for 20 years, and a 350cc Douglas Mark V motorbike from 1950 goes under the hammer.(n)

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a car auction business. A near-perfect 50s Chevy panel van and a very desirable low-mileage 80s Brooklands Capri tempt auctioneer Derek out of Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores nature's evolutionary anomalies and examines two creatures whose stretched features have given them an edge in the natural world. The chameleon has an extra-long tongue that helps it catch prey and the giraffe's neck is so long it is easily able to reach the tops of trees for food.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough highlights the curiosities that have led to accusations of forgery, but have ultimately helped assist the rethinking of evolution. When early explorers brought the first specimen of a duck-billed platypus to England in 1799, it was considered so bizarre it was deemed a hoax. Similarly, the midwife toad became the centre of a scientific storm in the 1920s that led to accusations of fakery.(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wildlife that inhabit remote islands, revealing the advantages and disadvantages of the creatures' habitat. Three-toed sloths in the Caribbean and nesting albatross enjoy a peaceful existence untroubled by predators, but other animals are not so fortunate. Marine iguanas in the Galapagos Islands are preyed on by racer snakes from birth, while penguins on the Antarctic island of Zavodovski risk being battered against the rocks by fierce waves.(n)

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: The first of three programmes about the wildlife, landscape and culture in different areas of the country explores the Sierra Madre mountains, and reveals how this rugged landscape has shaped the nation. This chain of peaks includes several volcanoes and is home to a wide variety of animals, including black bears, monarch butterflies, orchid bees and quetzals. The programme also visits the ruins of an ancient Aztec shrine and the blue agave plant which grows in the mountains is used in making tequila.(n)

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: Exploring the forests of the Yucatan Peninsula, which contain the ruins of the Mayan civilisation and a wide range of wildlife, including jaguars, monkeys and tropical birds. The region also holds a secret that is the key to how Mexico's people and animals are able to survive during the country's dry season.(n)

programme.title: Mexico: Earth's Festival of Life
programme.desc: The series concludes by exploring the north of the country, which is dominated by two deserts - the Sonoran and the Chihuahuan. The programme explores the conditions that have shaped this environment, and reveals how animals including prairie dogs, pygmy owls and aplomado falcons have adapted to thrive in these arid conditions.(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Footage of marine life in different environments around the north, beginning with a tropical coral reef which has medicinal properties for dolphins, and is used as a tool by tusk fish. In Japan, a shipwreck is home to the Asian sheepshead wrasse, which can change gender, while in the polar north, walruses struggle to find ice floes for their pups to rest on as climate change takes its toll on the environment.(n)

programme.title: Duran Duran: There's Something You Should Know
programme.desc: Simon Le Bon, John Taylor, Nick Rhodes and Roger Taylor open up about the highs and lows of the band's career - with archive footage of former guitarist Andy Taylor helping round out the story. The documentary charts their trajectory through seven of their 14 albums, each one uncovering a compelling chapter in the history of five lads from Birmingham who had one simple dream - to become the biggest band in the world. Contributors include Boy George, Nile Rodgers and Mark Ronson.(n)

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Railways
programme.sub-title: White Pass and Yukon Railroad - The Gold Rush Railway
programme.desc: The White Pass and Yukon Railroad over the US-Canada border, which began construction after the Klondike Gold Rush in the late 19th century and saw 35 workers lose their lives.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Railways
programme.sub-title: Kiruna to Narvik - Hitler's Arctic Railway Line
programme.desc: The Nordland Line, the longest in Norway at 453 miles. Stretching between Trondheim and Bodo, it was first built to transport the country's greatest natural resource - iron ore.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn explores St Pancras station in London and its sister hotel - built to show off the might of the Midlands, and learns about Castle Howard station in North Yorkshire.(n)

programme.title: Shipwreck Secrets
programme.desc: Divers search a newly discovered wreck as they investigate the story of the SS Justicia, sunk in the First World War by U-Boats off the coast of Ireland.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: Norman Conquest of Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to the south-west corner of Wales and one of Britain's finest coastal paths to find out why Pembrokeshire feels so English, even though it's more than 100 miles from the border. With its numerous castles and a fine cathedral, there are sure signs the Normans stamped their mark over the area. Tony's four-day walk traces the story of their conquest of the county, which he discovers was very different from the knockout victory at Hastings, involving a long, fiercely fought struggle.(n)

programme.title: History's Greatest Hoaxes
programme.desc: The stories of famous hoaxes, featuring personal accounts of those who perpetrated and exposed them, beginning with the infamous alien autopsy footage.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: Nazi Occupation - Channel Islands
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict.(n)

programme.title: History's Greatest Hoaxes

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Porridge: Inside Out
programme.desc: A look at the making of the prison sitcom, originally shown to coincide with its 40th anniversary. Writers Dick Clement and Ian La Frenais guide cameras around the pivotal places they visited during the creation and development of the series, including the pub where they first met, the prisons they researched, and the filming locations they used. Plus, contributions by the show's admirers, surviving members of the cast and members of the production crew.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.sub-title: Walking Through History: King John's Ruin - Peak District
programme.desc: As the 800th anniversary of the signing of Magna Carta in June 1215 approaches, Tony Robinson sets out to discover the real King John. His walk across the medieval monarch's stamping grounds of Sherwood Forest and the Peak District reveals a hapless man stumbling from crisis to crisis who, in the end, had no choice but to sign away some of his power. In the midst of what are now cultivated fields, Tony finds the scant remains of Laxton Castle, once the control centre where strict 'forest law' was administered and where taxes and fines flooded into the royal coffers. Later, in Sherwood, he explores how the myth of Robin Hood has helped to skew the public's image of King John.(n)

programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on another journey around the nation with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins by travelling from High Wycombe, Buckinghamshire, to Stratford-upon-Avon in Warwickshire, finding out about the remarkable craftsmen behind the Victorian furniture trade, discovering how George Bradshaw helped save Britain's canal heritage and seeing Shakespeare through the eyes of a 19th-century rail tourist.(n)

programme.desc: Mike Brewer sets out to buy a Ford Sierra Cosworth on a budget of only £5,000, before handing it over to mechanic Edd China for extensive renovation work.(n)

programme.title: Outback Opal Hunters

programme.title: Salvage Hunters

programme.title: How Do They Do It?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the first dinner date in Middlesbrough glamorous 41-year-old sales assistant Gail hosts a night full of sparkly gourmet grub and teaches her guests a lesson or two in beauty. Her guests are 52-year-old vintage shop owner Jane, 28-year-old football coach Dan, 57-year-old art enthusiast Julie and 47-year-old pub landlord Gary. Gail gets the glitter, sparklers and hair extensions out, but whether her food will get glowing reviews ramins to be seen.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Goalkeeping coach Dan hopes to thrill his guests with a James Bond evening in Middlesbrough, including 007-inspired food and a Bond villain. however, Julie and Jane fail to 'bond' on the night, falling out over everything from who's been the best James Bond, to how to pronounce Roger Moore. Gary and Gail also fall out when the table chat turns to 'lookey-likeys'. After dinner, Dan challenges his guests to put one past him in a penalty shoot-out, which ends with a surprising result.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Middlesbrough, pub landlord Gary plans a 1980s evening, serving classic dishes with a modern twist. However, Jane and Julie are still bickering and Gary falls out with Gail over his dessert. Showman Gary flambés his steaks at the table, and tops things off with a magic display that goes down well with all of his guests - except one.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in Middlesbrough, Julie's Indian menu gets mixed reviews, while Gail and Jane make a shoe discovery that leads to some shocking dinner table revelations. programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Vet Emily oversees a very tricky giraffe capture on the Eastern Cape, while Rachel is worried about orphan Lily's progress at the Care for Wild Africa rhino sanctuary.(n)

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: A pride of lions at the Sibuya Game Reserve that once ate some poachers need to be moved - but the task of transporting them to their new home is far from safe.(n)

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: At the Riverside Rehabilitation Centre, Debbie is put to the test as six baby monkeys escape high into a tree. Meanwhile, Emma greets a new baby mongoose.(n)

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Marine biologist Alison is busy preparing for the release of Nick, a critically endangered African penguin, on the Western Cape. Vet Emily has to anaesthetise a cheetah that needs to go to a new home.(n)

programme.title: Work on the Wild Side
programme.desc: Anna and the team from the Rhino Revolution dehorn two rare and very dangerous black rhinos, while Emma prepares Finya the black-backed jackal for release. Jenna and Sean, the leading forces in elephant research, tackle the growing problem of human and elephant conflict.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA

programme.title: True Stories
programme.sub-title: America's Animal Hoarder: Horror at the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of Terry Thompson, who released his collection of exotic animals onto the streets of Zanesville, Ohio, before killing himself in October 2011. The authorities were forced to shoot 48 of the dangerous creatures, including tigers, lions and bears, to bring the situation under control. Only six animals survived, and were taken to a local zoo. This film, first shown as part of the True Stories strand, explores the events in Thompson's past that led up to the incident, and includes interviews with the police officers involved.(n)

programme.title: Joe Lycett's Got Your Back
programme.desc: The comedian becomes the people's champion in an offbeat show investigating buyers' rights and fighting for justice. In this edition, Joe takes a very special look back at the series, featuring behind-the-scenes insights, never-before-seen moments, and his most outrageous victories to date.(n)

programme.title: Formula E: And We Go Green
programme.desc: Documentary about the ground-breaking electric car racing series. Featuring behind-the-scenes stories of the drivers and engineers who are reinventing motor sport for a sustainable future, and thrilling race footage from the 2017/18 championship.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the popular words-and-numbers quiz, as Sean Lock and David Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Lexicographer Susie Dent is joined by Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.(n)

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: As another season of love draws to a close, Fred Sirieix and his fellow cupids pull out all the stops in the hope of ending on a high. However, things do not get off to a great start for cat lover Zoe and her date Joseph, who makes a nervous first impression. Will a bit of witty repartee and the revelation about his own furry friend encourage her to let her guard down? Meanwhile, James has extended his stay in the hope of meeting his soulmate - and it seems that 6ft 6 Max from Oxford might just be the man for him.(n)

programme.title: My F-ing Tourette's Family
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives of Oxfordshire couple Hayley and Richard Davies-Monk, and their sons Spencer, 13, and Lewis, nine, who both have Tourette's syndrome The film shows the daily challenges of living with the neurological disorder, which for the boys is expressed in uncontrollable physical and verbal tics that can often include inappropriate or taboo phrases.(n)

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. In this first episode, Andrew Fraser, formerly one of Australia's foremost criminal defence lawyers, begins the reinvestigation into the Peter Falconio story, visits the Outback crime scene and speaks to a leading forensic scientist and witnesses from the time of the murder, who raise serious questions about evidence in the case.(n)

programme.title: A Very British Hotel Chain: Inside Best Western
programme.desc: Sales rep Mark has an ambitious plan to repurpose an old job centre as a hotel, while one of the chain's establishments in Edinburgh's city centre is assessed by a hotel inspector. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.desc: Following the complex build of an extraordinary home costing millions in Mayfair, London, which belongs to self-made billionaire and philanthropist John Caudwell. Having sold Phones 4u in 2006 for just under 1.5 billion pounds, John is now aiming to create one of the most lavish and expensive homes ever to be renovated in Britain. As the build enters the final crucial stages, the programme follows this unique and challenging engineering project as a team of expert trades people and interior designers attempt to pull off one of the most extraordinary renovations in Britain.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of engineer Richard Bennett and his partner Felicia Böhm as they transform an underground water reservoir near the Humber Estuary into a family home. programme.title: Private Benjamin (1980)
programme.desc: A scatterbrained society woman looks for a change of pace after her husband dies on their wedding night, and is tricked by a duplicitous recruiter into enlisting in the army. In spite of the initial culture shock and conflict with her tough commanding officer, she gradually finds direction and self-esteem for the first time in her life. Upon completion of basic training, she meets a dashing French doctor, who is in Biloxi for a medical conference. They part ways after a brief romance but it is not long before she must decide between her Army career or love. Comedy, starring Goldie Hawn, Armand Assante, Eileen Brennan, Harry Dean Stanton, Sam Wanamaker and Barbara Barrie.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, John Cassavetes, Myrna Loy and Blythe Danner.(n)

programme.title: Bull
programme.desc: The jury consultant helps a doctor who crossed state lines to purchase a marijuana derived oil to treat her patients in Virginia, where the oil is illegal. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green are called in to investigate the murder of a notorious bounty hunter. Piecing together his final movements, they turn their attentions to a meeting between the victim and an ambitious journalist, which may be able to shed light on what happened.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Logan and Briscoe investigate when members of a bachelor party watch an amateur video that appears to depict a girl's rape and murder. However, their inquiries reveal the apparent victim is alive, but has fallen foul of a points-for-sex club at her prestigious high-school.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: After a woman is killed during an attempt to shoot her husband, the team discover the couple were attending an infertility clinic where the doctor was involved in illegal practices. However, they find themselves unable to arrest him due to confidentiality rules, and his patients' reluctance to talk.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman's partially decomposed body is found in the Hudson River, and her husband initially becomes the prime suspect for her murder. However, as the investigation proceeds, Briscoe and Green uncover a related corruption scandal involving several lawyers and a judge. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Tensions are high between new partners Briscoe and Curtis as they investigate the murder of a wealthy student in a biker bar. The detectives discover the victim's father had offered him $10,000 to drop his hobby and his biker girlfriend. Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt star.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The murder of an undercover narcotics agent during a drugs raid splits Kincaid and McCoy over whether or not to ask for the newly reinstated death penalty. Ultimately, District Attorney Schiff must make the call. Crime drama, starring Sam Waterston and Jill Hennessy.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is made acting director while Jenny is attending an overseas conference. However, he decides to ignore his duties and investigate the murder of a man who arrived dead in a taxi at the team's headquarters with no clues to his identity - except a mysterious list of names. Drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs tries to find out why the agents are being forced by Homeland Security to take polygraph tests, while Tony and Jeanne are held hostage by a drug dealer. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: NCIS detectives uncover a bomb plot and a long-running theft ring while investigating a death at a Quantico reunion. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: When the NCIS team investigates a marine sergeant's fall to her death from a building, her doctor, Dr Grace Confalone, confides in Gibbs urging him to consider this a murder investigation. Meanwhile, Torres searches for a place to live.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Convicted felon Gabriel Hicks swears he was framed for murder a decade ago, so Gibbs begins his own investigation starting with a new autopsy by Ducky. They quickly determine two serious issues with the original findings - the killer was left-handed while Hicks is right-handed, and there was a mysterious witness who was never found. Meanwhile, FBI Agent Fornell joins the unit.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.desc: A challenge to Arthur from a mysterious warrior sets in motion a terrible chain of events, which leads to the uncovering of a dark secret that threatens the whole kingdom, as Merlin and the Prince respond to a request from the beautiful Morgause. Emilia Fox (Silent Witness) guest stars alongside Bradley James and Colin Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: The wizard rescues a druid girl who has been captured by a bounty hunter, but his heroics lead to the release of a ferocious magical beast that brings terror to Camelot. As Merlin tries to protect the stranger, their friendship develops, but the arrival of her former jailer forces him to make several heart-breaking decisions. Fantasy drama, starring Colin Morgan and Laura Donnelly.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Dignitaries from rival kingdoms visit Camelot for peace talks, but one monarch, King Alined, hopes to stir up war by using magic to bewitch Arthur into falling in love with Vivian, the daughter of King Olaf. As the prince grows besotted, chaos reigns and it seems inevitable that the lands will be pitched into conflict. Guest starring Georgia Moffett and Mark Lewis Jones, with Bradley James.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: A ruthless warlock sends Mordred to steal a priceless magical relic Uther has locked deep within Camelot's vaults, relying on Morgana's bond with the boy to keep him safe on his mission. Though many have died trying to get their hands on the Crystal of Neahtid in the past, the true power of the ancient artefact is beyond even Merlin's imagination. Joseph Mawle (Jesus in The Passion) guest stars, with Katie McGrath.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: Fearing the revival of the deadly Knights of Medhir after 300 years, Arthur and Merlin leave Camelot to fight the magical army. In their absence an enchantment is cast that subdues the whole population except Morgana, and Morgause returns to the all-but-defenceless city - more determined than ever to destroy Uther. Starring Emilia Fox, Bradley James, Colin Morgan and Katie McGrath.(n)

programme.title: Blood & Treasure
programme.desc: Danny poses as an arms dealer to extract information from a middleman used by terrorist Karim Farouk, while Lexi looks to Father Chuck for help deciphering information.(n)

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: A piece of salmon comes up positive for cocaine, and officers investigate when carpets from Syria have something suspicious woven through the fabric.(n)

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Officers investigate a man arriving from Beijing who has strong readings for narcotics on his belongings, while three men claim they are in Australia to visit a temple that does not appear to have been built yet. Plus, a mobile X-ray unit is needed to inspect some large rolls of fabric from Thailand.(n)

programme.title: Nothing to Declare
programme.desc: Another edition of the fly-on-the-wall documentary accessing the world of national security at Australia's biggest airports, following customs and immigration officers as they go about their daily business of tackling drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists.(n)

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: An away team encounters a powerful destructive force known as the Crystalline Entity while conducting a land survey on Melona Four. programme.title: Hawaii Five-0
programme.sub-title: Aohe mea make i ka hewa; make no i ka mihi ole (No One Has Ever Died for the Mistakes He Has Made; Only Because He Didn't Repent
programme.desc: A hitman comes to McGarrett to finally confess his crimes and admit where he buried his victims, while Jessie makes a dangerous decision that could ruin Adam's life.(n)

programme.title: Merlin
programme.desc: The Great Dragon is finally free, and attacks Camelot. With the castle crumbling, the knights cannot hold out much longer - only a Dragonlord can kill such a beast and Uther wiped them all out years ago, or so it is believed. Gaius reveals one such man may still be alive and in hiding, so Arthur and Merlin set out in search of him, before the kingdom is destroyed. But even if they find him, can they persuade him to help his old enemy Uther? Colin Morgan, Bradley James, Richard Wilson and Anthony Head star, with a guest appearance by John Lynch.(n)

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: A profile of serial killer Danny Rolling, nicknamed the Gainesville Ripper, who targeted Florida students with crimes so horrific that he inspired the movie Scream.(n)

programme.title: Fred Dinenage: Murder Casebook
programme.desc: Fred Dinenage offers an insight into the life of Lord Lucan, suspected of murdering his children's nanny, Sandra Rivett, in November 1974. The peer subsequently disappeared and has never been seen since, though unsubstantiated rumours suggest he died in Africa in 2004.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Crime Scene Investigation
programme.desc: A murder in the small Nevada town of Jackpot leads Grissom to discover certain local people do not want the crime to be solved. Undeterred, he meets the person who found the victim's severed head in woodland, and evidence leads him to the rest of the body - buried in a shallow grave. Drama, starring William Petersen, Marg Helgenberger and Gary Dourdan.(n)

programme.desc: A cynical road-repairer falls for the alluring charms of an Italian girl working as a barmaid, only to discover she and her family are planning to move to Canada. Romantic drama written by Emeric Pressburger, starring John Gregson, Belinda Lee and Cyril Cusack.(n)

programme.title: The October Man (1947)
programme.desc: A chemist with suicidal tendencies is falsely accused of murder - and his precarious mental state is further upset by taunts from the real killer. Psychological thriller, adapted from a novel by Eric Ambler, starring John Mills, Joan Greenwood and Joyce Carey.(n)

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest (2002)
programme.desc: Two bachelors using the same pseudonym capture the hearts of a couple of girls during a delightful weekend in the countryside. However, their cunning deception leads to all manner of hilarious confusion as the ladies in question attempt to work out who is who. Adaptation of Oscar Wilde's comedy, starring Rupert Everett, Colin Firth, Frances O'Connor, Reese Witherspoon, Judi Dench and Tom Wilkinson.(n)

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: Seetha Hallett helps house-hunters looking for a property on the coast to find their dream home.(n)

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.sub-title: Give a Cat Two New Feet - Done!
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the work of 'bionic' vet Noel Fitzpatrick, who uses pioneering surgical techniques at his state-of-the-art centre in Surrey to save the lives of critically ill pets from around the country. In the first episode he tries to replace a cat's two paws after they were severed by a combine harvester, and tackles a Labrador's crippling arthritis.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.(n)

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the comedy. Gary and Dermot worry about who will moving into the newly vacant flat above them, but their fears prove unfounded when it turns out to be an attractive blonde.(n)

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot and Gary get serious over a chess contest in which the victor gains an evening of pleasure. Laddish comedy, starring Martin Clunes and Leslie Ash.(n)

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed
programme.desc: Two teenage shoplifters are caught by the police, and cricketer Andrew Flintoff receives the honour of officially opening a new store, but there is a race to get the publicity photos to the papers on time. Meanwhile, people use a roof top to make urban honey, and a woman is injured.(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments.(n)

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson.(n)

programme.title: Blood Feud (2016)
programme.desc: An ex-convict is released from 10 years in prison for killing a gangster's son - unaware that his best friend has been hired by his victim's father to murder him. When the truth emerges, the two old partners in crime decide to work together to make amends for their past misdeeds - even if it means crossing the vengeful crime boss. Crime drama, starring Paul King and Micky Curate.(n)

programme.title: Bread (1971)
programme.desc: A group of friends camps out on what turns out to be private property, and gets coerced into redecorating the owner's house in return for being allowed to stay. The squatters plan to stage a music festival without the landowner finding out - and shoot a porn movie to raise the money. Drama, starring Anthony Nigel and Peter Marinker.(n)

programme.title: Burning an Illusion (1981)
programme.desc: A black woman living in West London hopes to marry her boyfriend, but finds her life thrown into upheaval when he loses his job and is subsequently arrested after getting into a fight. The experience makes her reassess her priorities, and she decides to reconnect with her African heritage. Drama, starring Cassie McFarlane and Victor Romero Evans.(n)

He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice.(n)

programme.title: Meet the Lords
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over a year following life at the House of Lords, one of Britain's oldest, most idiosyncratic and most important institutions, going behind the scenes during a turbulent 12 months that has seen dramatic changes in the political landscape and the Lords battling it out with the Government. The first edition introduces the larger-than-life characters that populate the House, including new people's peers, hereditaries and political grandees, as they try to change legislation and follow their personal passions.(n)

programme.title: Callan
programme.desc: Spy drama, starring Edward Woodward as an embittered secret agent.(n)

programme.title: London Town (2016)
programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy living in 1970s London has his life changed forever when his is introduced to The Clash by his estranged mother. Drama, starring Tom Hughes, Jonathan Rhys Meyers and Dougray Scott.(n)

programme.title: Brimstone and Treacle (1982)
programme.desc: Asinister stranger worms his way into a middle-aged couple's home by offering to take care of their comatose daughter - but it is only once he's part of the family that his true nature shines through. Drama based on Dennis Potter's TV play, starring Sting, Denholm Elliott, Joan Plowright and Suzanna Hamilton.(n)

programme.title: Anchoress (1993)
programme.desc: A peasant girl develops a fascination with a statue of the Virgin Mary, which prompts a priest to sentence her to be walled up in the church. When she discovers her mother is to be tried as a witch, she resolves to break free from her imprisonment, even if it costs her her life. Medieval drama, starring Natalie Morse, Toyah Willcox, Pete Postlethwaite and Christopher Eccleston.(n)

programme.title: 3 Steps to Heaven (1995)
programme.desc: A man's body is found in the Thames, and his girlfriend is convinced he was murdered. Adopting a series of disguises, she sets out to find the last three men to see him alive, and discover the truth about what happened - but is unprepared for the secrets she uncovers about the man she thought she knew. Drama, starring Katrin Cartlidge, Frances Barber and James Fleet.(n)

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes over the kitchen of Kidbrooke School in Greenwich, hoping to re-educate the junk food-loving children by introducing a healthy diet to improve concentration and eliminate long-term problems of obesity and heart disease. He joins forces with dinner lady Nora in his Feed Me Better campaign, aiming to promote nutritious dishes throughout the capital's schools before encouraging the rest of the country to follow suit.(n)

programme.title: Man Friday (1975)
programme.desc: Shipwrecked mariner Robinson Crusoe is overjoyed to discover he has a fellow castaway, a black islander whom he names Friday, and insists in treating as a servant and trying to convert to Western culture. Friday quickly grasps that Crusoe will never see him as an equal, and uses his cunning to turns the tables on the Englishman. Comedy, starring Peter O'Toole and Richard Roundtree.(n)

programme.title: Portrait from Life (1949)
programme.desc: A British army officer searches for a Jewish refugee in post-war Germany, only to discover that she has lost her memory. Romantic mystery, starring Robert Beatty and Mai Zetterling.(n)

programme.title: Churchill's First World War
programme.desc: Drama-documentary charting Winston Churchill's road to redemption after his disgrace following the Dardanelles disaster during the First World War through intimate letters he wrote to his wife Clementine. It reveals how he was instrumental in the creation of the tank and notes his contribution to the Allied victory in 1918 as Minister of Munitions, demonstrating how the future prime minister was shaped by his experiences of the Great War.(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner.(n)
